game_description: Compete to terraform Mars through engine-building and card combos.

strategies.strategy_detailed: Corporations define your engine. Ecoline, Inventrix, and Teractor are strong general-purpose picks. Avoid corporations that depend on specific project card combos unless you're experienced — their power spikes are hard to set up under time pressure.

Keep projects that cost 10 MC or less and directly produce resources or TR. Expensive projects (20+ MC) are only worth keeping if they're central to your corporation's synergy. Average keep rate is 3-4 cards from 10.

TR is both your score and your production ceiling. Falling behind on TR means fewer MC per generation. Prioritize green projects (plants, oceans, temperature) that advance TR even if they're slightly expensive. A 2-TR bump is worth more than 6 MC saved.

Prioritize cards that increase steel, titanium, or energy production in generation 1-3. Steel and titanium discount building and space projects respectively — a 5-production boost saves 5+ MC per turn. Compound production gains over energy or heat.

Milestones give 5 VP for 8 MC — the best point-per-credit purchase in the game. Terraformer (TR 25), Gardener (5 greeneries), and Builder (8 building tags) are most achievable. If you're within 2 projects of a milestone, prioritize it over almost everything.

When the global parameters stall, your TR gain drops sharply. Force progress with greenery placements (raises oxygen) or thermal capacity projects. Don't let the game end too slowly — stalled parameters let opponents engine-build and catch up on VP.
strategy_stealth: Stalled parameters let opponents catch up

strategies.strategy_detailed: Fund the award you're currently leading by the widest margin. Awards cost 8-24 MC depending on order — the first funder pays 8 MC for 5 VP if they win. Banker (MC production), Landlord (tiles), and Scientist (tags) are reliably contestable.
strategy_stealth: Fund award you lead by most
First funder pays 8 MC for 5 VP

strategies.strategy_detailed: Greeneries placed adjacent to your cities score 1 VP per adjacent city at game end. Plan city and greenery placements to cluster — each adjacency is worth 1 VP to you. Opponent cities adjacent to your greeneries score them 1 VP too, so block if cheap.

If you're drawing 3+ cards per generation through research actions, evaluate your hand quality. A full hand of unplayable expensive projects is worse than a smaller focused hand. Use the standard action to buy 1 card only if it completes a combo or hits a milestone.

Count remaining parameter steps. If ahead on VP, slow the game — skip parameters you don't benefit from. If trailing, accelerate: place greeneries, raise temperature, trigger oceans to end before opponents complete their engines.
strategy_stealth: Ahead: slow parameters you don't need

strategies.strategy_detailed: A TR gap of 5+ in the final two generations is nearly unrecoverable through projects alone. Pivot to VP actions: awards, milestones, tile placement, and high-VP projects. Chasing TR at this point costs more MC than it returns in points.
strategy_stealth: TR gap 5+: pivot to VP actions

strategies.condition: Evaluating projects vs TR in final gen
strategies.strategy_detailed: In the last generation, each MC is worth exactly (VP per MC) of its best use. Compare: 1 greenery = 1 TR + 1 potential VP adjacency. A 12 MC project giving 3 VP = 0.25 VP/MC. Standard greenery is often the highest VP-per-MC play in the final generation.
strategy_stealth: Final gen: greenery = TR + adjacency VP
Compare VP per MC for each action
